skill.name: notebooklm-manager
skill.description: Interact with registered NotebookLM notebooks through Claude Code Chrome integration. The skill supports listing, adding, querying, searching, enabling, disabling, and removing notebook entries.
skill.summary: Manage and query NotebookLM notebooks from Claude Code using browser automation.
skill.author: LeeJuOh

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks, table text, and JSON examples rather than executable code. The meaningful risks are intended browser automation against NotebookLM and local registry writes, so publication is acceptable with a medium warning.

medium_findings.title: Browser Automation Sends User Queries to NotebookLM
medium_findings.description: The skill delegates notebook URLs and user questions to a Chrome subagent that interacts with NotebookLM. This is the intended function, but user prompts and notebook context may be processed by Google NotebookLM through the signed-in browser session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Task prompt explicitly passes URL and Question fields to a browser automation subagent. This confirms external service interaction, but it matches the stated purpose and is not evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Notebook Registry Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill is allowed to write local data files under its skill data directory for notebook indexes, archives, and metadata. This is scoped storage behavior, but users should understand that notebook URLs and metadata are persisted locally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools include Write and the storage section defines concrete local files to create. The paths are within SKILL_ROOT data storage, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections occur in markdown code spans, command names, and illustrative Task examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are rendered documentation text and fenced examples, not source code that can execute. The repository contains only markdown files for this skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography lines are description text, labels, table headers, or JSON metadata examples. No cryptographic API, digest generation, or password hashing operation appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain markdown or sample data, and there is no implementation code using MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms. This makes the scanner result a high-confidence false positive.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance findings are notebook URL parsing and example workflow text. No command gathers host, network, process, or environment information from the user system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe extracting a NotebookLM ID from a URL and reading the skill registry. They do not perform system enumeration or collect machine information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Delegated Browser Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill invokes a named subagent through Task for Chrome-based NotebookLM interaction. Delegation is purposeful, but it expands the operational boundary beyond local markdown instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Task calls are explicit and repeated for initial and follow-up queries. The behavior is transparent in the skill text, so the concern is boundary awareness rather than hidden execution.

content.user_title: Manage NotebookLM Notebooks from Claude Code
content.value_statement: NotebookLM users need a consistent way to find, query, and maintain notebook references during research work. This skill provides Claude Code workflows for NotebookLM registry management and browser-assisted querying.
actual_capabilities: Lists active NotebookLM notebooks from a local registry.
Adds NotebookLM URLs with optional browser-based metadata discovery.
Queries a selected notebook through a Chrome automation subagent.
Searches registered notebooks by ID, title, or topics.
Enables, disables, removes, and archives notebook records.
Tracks notebook metadata in local JSON files under the skill data directory.
limitations: Requires Claude Code with Chrome integration and a connected browser session.
Works only with NotebookLM URLs and registered NotebookLM notebooks.
Cannot perform general web search or local document analysis outside NotebookLM.
NotebookLM access depends on the user being signed in to the required Google account.

use_cases.title: Query Research Notebooks
use_cases.description: Ask a registered NotebookLM notebook targeted questions and receive answers with source-oriented formatting.

use_cases.title: Maintain Notebook Indexes
use_cases.description: Add, search, disable, or remove notebook records without manually editing registry files.

use_cases.title: Check Documentation Collections
use_cases.description: Use NotebookLM as a source-backed documentation reference while working inside Claude Code.

prompt_templates.title: Ask a Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask my claude-docs notebook what changed in the latest setup workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Add Notebook URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Add this NotebookLM URL and discover its title and topics: https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/example

prompt_templates.title: Compare Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Query my product-research notebook about pricing, onboarding, and enterprise requirements. Follow up until each topic is covered.

output_examples.output: A concise list of active notebook IDs, names, topics, and status values from the local registry.

output_examples.input: Ask my policy notebook what it says about data retention.
output_examples.output: A NotebookLM answer with the notebook name, synthesized response, citations, and suggested follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Add this NotebookLM URL and discover its topics.
output_examples.output: A confirmation that includes the generated notebook ID, title, detected topics, and a short discovered-content summary.

best_practices: Use clear notebook IDs so follow-up prompts resolve to the intended NotebookLM notebook.
Confirm whether chat history should be cleared before sensitive first queries.
Review locally stored notebook URLs and metadata before sharing the skill workspace.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for general web searches or non-NotebookLM research.
Do not register notebooks that contain data you are not allowed to process in NotebookLM.
Do not assume browser automation works without an active Chrome connection and valid login.

faq.question: Does this skill access NotebookLM directly?
faq.answer: It delegates browser interaction to a Chrome automation subagent. The user must have the required browser integration connected.

faq.question: Where are notebook records stored?
faq.answer: Notebook IDs, URLs, topics, and metadata are stored in local JSON files under the skill data directory.

faq.question: Can it query notebooks that are not registered?
faq.answer: A NotebookLM URL can be added first. After registration, the skill can query it by its generated ID.

faq.question: Does it clear NotebookLM chat history automatically?
faq.answer: No. It asks before the first query to a notebook in the current conversation.

faq.question: Can it replace NotebookLM source citations?
faq.answer: No. It asks NotebookLM and formats returned answers, but source quality depends on the notebook contents.

faq.question: Is this safe for confidential notebooks?
faq.answer: Use caution. Queries and notebook content may be processed by NotebookLM through the signed-in browser session.
